title: Box 1
description: Box<T> allocates data on the heap. It's useful for recursive types and large data that you don't want on the stack.
instructions: Use Box to create a recursive data structure.
hints: Rust needs to know the size of every type at compile time.
A recursive type has infinite size unless you add indirection.
Wrap the recursive field in a Box: Cons(i32, Box<List>)
explanation: Box<T> is the simplest smart pointer - it allocates data on the heap and owns it. For recursive types like a linked list, the compiler can't determine the type's size because it's self-referential. Box provides a pointer of known size (one pointer width), breaking the infinite recursion. Box::new() allocates on the heap and returns the box.
